results.title: E25 Bell's palsy
results.raw_output:
EXPLANATION: Your symptoms – facial weakness on one side, difficulty closing your eye, and a drooping mouth – strongly suggest Ramsay Hunt syndrome, which is caused by reactivation of the varicella-zoster virus (the chickenpox virus). It often presents with facial paralysis and a rash. While your vital signs and bloodwork are normal, the facial weakness is concerning. The mild cold you had recently could be related. It’s important to rule out this possibility and receive appropriate treatment promptly.

results.title: F25 Bell's palsy
results.raw_output:
EXPLANATION: The symptoms you’re describing – facial weakness, difficulty closing your eye, and a drooping mouth – strongly suggest Bell’s palsy. This condition is often triggered by a viral infection, like the cold you recently had. It causes inflammation of the facial nerve, which controls muscle movement on one side of the face. While it’s frequently temporary and resolves on its own, it’s important to rule out other potential causes and get a diagnosis. The lack of other symptoms like headache or limb weakness is reassuring.
